clinical_workflow.intended_use: Clinician-controlled documentation scaffold for tinnitus impact review.
clinical_workflow.not_for: Not for autonomous diagnosis, treatment selection, dosing, disposition, admission/discharge, procedural, imaging, referral, or emergency management decisions.

source_phase1.reason_for_inclusion: Common ENT documentation scenario suitable as clinician-controlled note scaffolding rather than autonomous clinical advice.
source_phase1.distinct_purpose: Distinct documentation workflow for clinician-selected details around tinnitus impact review.
source_phase1.duplicate_check_notes: Cleaned Phase 1 candidate. Exact ID is unique within this revised index and must be checked against existing_workflow_ids.json and alias inventory before import; no close baseline workflow assigned in this cleaned index.

safety_notes: No autonomous diagnosis or treatment recommendation is intended.
Red flags are documentation prompts only.
red_flag_prompts: severe or rapidly worsening symptoms documented if assessed
abnormal vital signs documented if measured
new neurological, cardiorespiratory, or systemic concern documented if assessed
clinician concern requiring escalation documented if present

chips.text: Tinnitus impact review interval history documented
onset/duration documented if discussed
chips.evidence_relationship: The exact section supports interval tinnitus experience, duration, concerns, hearing and noise context, sleep impact, preferences and discussion without asserting a cause.

chips.text: change since last review documented
chips.evidence_relationship: The exact section supports interval tinnitus experience, duration, concerns, hearing and noise context, sleep impact, preferences and discussion without asserting a cause.

chips.text: severity/impact on function documented if discussed

history_draft.text: Tinnitus impact review. [Interval history]. [Current symptoms/concerns]. [Change since last review]. [Relevant negatives]. [Clinician-documented discussion]. [Additional history].

exam_prompts.text: Documented if assessed or measured. Vital signs documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Ear/nose/throat examination documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Neck examination documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. General appearance documented.

investigation_options.text: Reviewed if relevant. Reviewed. Clinician-entered Audiology or tympanometry results reviewed.
Reviewed if relevant. Reviewed. Clinician-entered Nasal/throat swab or culture results reviewed.
investigation_options.output_text: Nasal/throat swab or culture results reviewed.

investigation_options.text: Reviewed if relevant. Reviewed. Clinician-entered Imaging or specialist report reviewed.

plan_options.text: Safety-netting documented if discussed by clinician.
Follow-up documented if arranged by clinician.
Patient questions or counseling discussion documented if discussed.
plan_options.output_text: Patient questions or counseling discussion documented.

clinical_blockers: Exact tinnitus type, laterality, pulsatility, mental-health risk, test results, diagnosis, and management remain clinician-entered.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
